title: Thioacylation of the amino group of an amino acid
abstract:
Abstract
               The problems of side-reactions encountered in acylation and alkoxycarbonylation of amino acids (Chapter 2) are not encountered in thioacylation protocols Thioacylation of amines is easily accomplished with thionesters although the reaction rates are relatively slow; the corresponding reaction, aminolysis of carboxylic esters, can be used in acylation but is slower still. The choice of the chloride leaving group was a logical improvement for the provision of efficient acylating agents (a choice that is not available, for reasons of stability of reagents, for thioacylation), although the good leaving group propensity of the thiolate anion in nucleophilic substitution reactions continues to be exploited in N-trifluoroacetylation (Table 2.11 in Chapter 2) for which the commonly-used reagent is methyl trifluorothiolacetate, CF COSMe.
